codeReview.summary: Commit ec5bc86 correctly collapses frontend MappingRequest/MappingResponse to MappingDto and updates all affected type call sites in hooks, components, mapping helpers, and tests. The compare payload builder emits column_mappings entries with file_a_column/file_b_column/mapping_type and optional similarity only, matching the requested wire shape. I found no code-level correctness issues in scope.

sharedStateObservations.observation: This feature is marked completed but has no dedicated worker session/handoff and was bundled into an unrelated m1 tooling commit, which weakens feature-level traceability against the mission's commit hygiene rules.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Mission features entry for m4-collapse-mapping-request-response-frontend shows "workerSessionIds": [] and says "COMPLETED early in commit ec5bc86"; the commit's handoff is for featureId "m1-frontend-oxlint-setup" (handoffs/...f9688287...json lines 4 and 6); mission AGENTS.md line 48 states "One feature = one commit".

sharedStateObservations.observation: The carried-over handoff for commit ec5bc86 explicitly skipped agent-browser, so there is no direct interactiveChecks evidence attached to this feature's VAL-MAP-025 wire-shape assertion.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: validation-contract.md lines 248-251 define VAL-MAP-025 with Tool: agent-browser; handoff 2026-04-17T23-19-15-318Z__m1-frontend-oxlint-setup__f968... line 64 says "I did not run agent-browser."

summary: Reviewed feature implementation via commit ec5bc86 and associated transcript skeleton from the originating worker session. The MappingDto collapse and call-site updates are correct with no blocking/non-blocking code defects found; review passes, with traceability and validation-evidence process gaps noted in sharedStateObservations.
